title: Week of April 6, 2026
summary: This week focused on making OpsPal more reliable and self-maintaining. The platform now automatically cleans up outdated files and unused connections, fixes missing dependencies without requiring manual intervention, and prevents common Salesforce setup errors that could block your workflows.

entries.headline: Platform automatically cleans up old files during updates
details: Removes outdated cache files older than 7 days
Clears stuck session files older than 5 minutes
Alerts you if memory usage files become too large

entries.headline: Platform removes broken connections automatically
details: Removes dead scripts that could cause errors

entries.headline: Unused plugins removed automatically when starting sessions
Salesforce startup process now runs more smoothly
details: Migrates old connection settings to new format
Adds quality checking for Salesforce architecture

entries.headline: Critical Salesforce workflow fixes prevent common errors
details: Prevents field addition failures when Dynamic Forms are active
Automatically assigns record types to admin profiles
Stops routing issues from blocking your sessions

entries.headline: Missing dependencies install automatically at startup
details: Captures installation progress and error details
Shows manual fix commands when auto-install fails
Runs background fixes for safe dependency issues

entries.headline: False missing package errors no longer block sessions
